headline: Michael Payne on power, politics and the making of modern sport
summary: The former IOC marketing chief and adviser to Formula 1 reflects on five decades at the centre of global sport
body_text:
Michael Payne is one of the most influential figures of the modern sports business. A former director of marketing at the International Olympic Committee, he played a central role in creating the IOC’s worldwide sponsorship programme and has advised governing bodies and governments on major events. After leaving the IOC, Payne became special adviser to former Formula 1 supremo Bernie Ecclestone and now leads Payne Sports Media Strategies, advising sports organisations and governments worldwide.

In his newly published autobiography, Fast Tracks and Dark Deals, Payne gives an insider’s account of more than five decades at the intersection of sport, commerce and politics.

Samuel Agini: You have published a true insider story on fast tracks and dark deals. Why did you decide to publish it now? Did you keep notes all these years and what does it really say about the evolution of the global sports landscape and business?

Michael Payne: I was doing research on the sports business industry
